quiz.title: Grade-10 Physics: Unit 6 Quiz 8
quiz.subtitle: Diffraction
quiz.subject: Physics
quiz.topic: Grade 10 - Waves and Simple Harmonic Motion

questions.question: Which of the following describes diffraction? (Select all that apply)
options: Bouncing of waves off a surface
Bending of waves entering a new medium
Combining of two waves to form a new pattern
questions.explanation: Diffraction is the spreading of waves through gaps or around obstacles. C is reflection, D is refraction, E is interference.

questions.question: Maximum diffraction occurs when: (Select all that apply)
options: The gap size is approximately equal to the wavelength
The obstacle size is approximately equal to the wavelength
The gap size is much larger than the wavelength
The wavelength is much smaller than the gap size
The wave speed is at its maximum
questions.explanation: Diffraction is greatest when the gap or obstacle size is comparable to the wavelength. C and D produce minimal diffraction. E is irrelevant.

questions.question: Diffraction is minimal when:
options: The wavelength is much smaller than the gap
The wavelength is equal to the gap size
The wavelength is much larger than the gap
The wavelength is comparable to the gap size
questions.explanation: Diffraction is minimal when the wavelength is much smaller than the size of the opening or obstacle. Diffraction becomes more noticeable when the wavelength is equal to, larger than, or comparable to the size of the gap.

questions.question: Sound waves diffract more than light waves because: (Select all that apply)
options: Sound wavelengths are comparable to everyday openings
Sound is longitudinal and light is transverse
Sound has higher frequency than light
questions.explanation: Wavelength size determines diffraction, not wave type, speed, or frequency. Sound wavelengths match everyday gaps.

questions.question: You hear someone in the next room but cannot see them because: (Select all that apply)
options: Sound waves have longer wavelengths than light
Sound travels faster in air than light
Sound waves have higher amplitude than light
questions.explanation: Longer sound wavelengths diffract around door-sized openings. Light's tiny wavelengths produce minimal diffraction.

questions.question: Water waves entering a harbor through a narrow gap: (Select all that apply)
options: Spread out in a circular pattern
questions.explanation: Spreading through a narrow gap is diffraction. No reflection, frequency change, or wavelength change occurs.

questions.question: To increase diffraction through a gap, you can: (Select all that apply)
questions.explanation: Narrower gaps and longer wavelengths both increase diffraction. Wider gaps, shorter wavelengths, and higher speed reduce or don't affect it.

questions.question: When a gap is much wider than the wavelength: (Select all that apply)
options: Waves pass through with little spreading
The frequency of the waves changes
questions.explanation: Wide gaps produce minimal diffraction. No reflection, frequency change, or amplitude doubling occurs.

questions.question: For a fixed gap, which diffracts more? (Select all that apply)
questions.explanation: Low frequency means long wavelength, which diffracts more. Wave speed does not affect the amount of diffraction.

questions.question: Light through a single narrow slit produces: (Select all that apply)
options: A perfect shadow of the slit
questions.explanation: The single-slit diffraction pattern has a bright central maximum with alternating dark and bright fringes on either side.

questions.question: Diffraction increases when: (Select all that apply)
options: The wavelength is close to the gap size
The wavelength is much smaller than the gap
questions.explanation: Longer wavelengths and wavelengths matching the gap size increase diffraction. Shorter wavelengths and higher frequencies reduce it.

questions.question: Which of the following use diffraction? (Select all that apply)
questions.explanation: Both describe X-ray diffraction used to study crystal structures. Sonar and ultrasound use reflection; fiber optics uses total internal reflection.

scoreMessages.90: Excellent! You've mastered the principles of diffraction!
scoreMessages.70: Good work! Review the relationship between wavelength, gap size, and the amount of diffraction.
scoreMessages.50: Keep practicing! Focus on why sound diffracts more than light and the conditions for maximum diffraction.
scoreMessages.0: Review the basics: definition of diffraction, conditions for maximum vs. minimal diffraction, and real-world examples.
